code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test23-MAY-2025Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low-normal urea, and mild hypoglycemia, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no hematuria or leukocyturia. Findings are consistent with possible renal dysfunction (e.g., glomerular disease or diabetic nephropathy) and warrant further nephrological evaluation.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
